valuation.system: Valuation Office Agency (VOA) banding on 1 April 1991 values

liability.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Part 1, ss.6-9
liability.joint_and_several: Where multiple people fall at the same hierarchy level, each is jointly and severally liable for the full amount.

special_cases.hmo: Houses in Multiple Occupation — owner is the liable person, not individual tenants.
special_cases.care_homes: Resident is liable unless a qualifying exemption applies.

discounts.name: Single Person Discount
discounts.eligibility: Only one person aged 18 or over lives in the property as their sole or main residence
discounts.eligibility_note: Some people are 'disregarded' and don't count as adults for Council Tax purposes

discounts.name: Severely Mentally Impaired Discount
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2(7)
discounts.effect: Either 25% or 100% depending on household composition
eligibility: Person is severely mentally impaired (certified by doctor)
Person receives qualifying benefit
discounts.evidence_required: Medical certificate from doctor confirming severe mental impairment
qualifying_benefits: Disability Living Allowance (middle or higher rate care component)
Universal Credit (limited capability for work element)

discounts.name: Care Leavers Discount
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Section 13(1)(c) — discretionary reduction

eligibility.age_history: Original scheme (April 2019) covered 18–21 only. Extended to 18–24 by Cabinet 10 January 2024, effective 1 April 2024.
eligibility.care_leaver_definition: Formerly a child in care of Gloucestershire County Council OR any other local authority in England, and became a 'Former Relevant Child' under Children (Leaving Care) Act 2000.
eligibility.residency: Must be resident and liable for Council Tax in Gloucester City Council area.
eligibility.liability_requirement: Must be the liable person — bill must be in their name. No discount where resident is in HMO and not the Council Tax payer.
eligibility.verification: Care leaver status confirmed by Gloucestershire County Council's 11-25 Permanency Service, who will seek confirmation from other local authorities where needed.
eligibility.geographic_scope: Applies to Former Relevant Children of any local authority in England (not limited to Gloucestershire CC — extended April 2024).

discounts.name: Carer Disregard
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2(5)
discounts.effect: Carer is disregarded - may result in 25% discount if only one other adult
eligibility: Provides care for at least 35 hours per week
Person cared for receives qualifying benefit
Person cared for is NOT your spouse, partner, or child under 18

discounts.name: Full-time Student Disregard
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2(4)
discounts.effect: Students disregarded - 100% discount if all adults are students, otherwise may result in 25% if one student with one other adult
eligibility: Enrolled on full-time course of education
Course lasts at least one academic year
At least 24 weeks per year
At least 21 hours study per week during term time

discounts.name: Apprentice and Youth Trainee Disregard
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2(6)
discounts.effect: Disregarded - may result in discount depending on household

eligibility.apprentices: Undertaking apprenticeship under Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009

discounts.name: School/College Leaver Disregard (Age 18-19)
discounts.eligibility: Left school or college after 30 April, aged 18-19

discounts.name: Disabled Band Reduction
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Section 13(2) and Schedule 1A
discounts.effect: One band lower (Band A gets 1/9th reduction)
eligibility: Property is main home of disabled person (adult or child)
Property has been adapted or has features essential for disabled person's needs

discounts.name: Empty Property Discount (Unoccupied and Unfurnished)
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1A, Paragraph 4

discounts.name: Major Repair Discount
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1A, Paragraph 4
discounts.eligibility: Property unoccupied and undergoing major structural alteration or repair to make it habitable

discounts.name: Annexe Discount
eligibility: Annexe occupied by relative of council tax payer of main property, OR
Annexe used as part of main property (not separately let)

discounts.name: Job-Related Second Home Discount
eligibility: Employer requires you to live in property (job-related accommodation)
You are also liable for Council Tax on a second furnished property
discounts.eligibility_note: Applies to the furnished second home, not the job-related property

discounts.name: Hospital/Care Patients
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2
discounts.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
eligibility: Long-term hospital patients
Residential care/nursing home residents
Hospice residents
Hostel residents (providing high level of care)

discounts.name: Persons in Detention
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2
discounts.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
eligibility: Persons in prison
Persons on remand
Persons detained under Mental Health Act 1983

discounts.name: Members of Religious Communities
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2
discounts.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
discounts.eligibility: Members of religious communities whose principal occupation is prayer, contemplation, education, or relief of suffering

discounts.name: Foreign Spouses/Dependants of Students
discounts.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992, Schedule 1, Paragraph 2
discounts.effect: Disregarded - not counted as adults for Council Tax
discounts.eligibility: Non-British spouses/dependants of students prevented by Home Office from working or claiming benefits

exemptions.name: Death of liable person (deceased estate)
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property unoccupied since death of liable person. No other joint owners or tenants. Probate/letters of administration not yet granted, or granted within last 6 months.
exemptions.duration: Indefinite until probate granted, then up to 6 months after grant
exemptions.note: Where there are other residents, no exemption applies. Death of one joint owner does not trigger Class F.

exemptions.name: Repossessed property
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Mortgagee (e.g. building society) has taken possession of unoccupied property under mortgage terms
exemptions.note: Property must be unoccupied. Previously in schema as Class B — corrected: Class B is charity-owned empty property.

exemptions.name: Charity-owned empty property
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property owned by a charity, unoccupied, last used for charitable purposes

exemptions.name: Last occupier receiving care (not in care home)
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — last occupier has moved elsewhere (not a care home or hospital) to receive personal care due to old age, disablement, illness, alcohol or drug dependence, or mental disorder

exemptions.name: Last occupier now in care home or hospital
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — last occupier's sole or main residence is now a hospital, care home or nursing home where they are receiving care

exemptions.name: Last occupier providing care elsewhere
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — last occupier has moved elsewhere to provide personal care to another person due to old age, disablement, illness, alcohol or drug dependence, or mental disorder

exemptions.name: Last occupier in prison or detention
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — liable person is detained in prison, hospital by court order, or immigration detention. Does not apply if detained for non-payment of council tax or a fine.

exemptions.name: Last occupier is a student
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — last occupier is a student and has been a student for the whole period since leaving, or became a student within 6 weeks of leaving

exemptions.name: Occupation prohibited by law
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — occupation prohibited by law or by action of a public body (e.g. closing order, demolition order, planning condition)

exemptions.name: Awaiting occupation by minister of religion
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied dwelling held for occupation by a minister of religion as a residence from which to perform their duties

exemptions.name: Armed forces accommodation (MOD-owned)
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Living accommodation owned by the Secretary of State for Defence, held for the purposes of armed forces accommodation. Applies whether occupied or unoccupied.

exemptions.name: Trustee in bankruptcy
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied — property is in the hands of a qualifying person in their capacity as Trustee in Bankruptcy under the Insolvency Act 1986 or Bankruptcy Act 1914. Applies even if jointly owned.
exemptions.note: Does not cover unoccupied dwellings where the liable person is a company in liquidation

exemptions.name: Annexe that cannot be let separately
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Unoccupied annexe forming part of a single property that includes another dwelling, which cannot be let separately without a breach of planning control (s.171A Town and Country Planning Act 1990)
exemptions.duration: While unoccupied and planning restriction continues
exemptions.note: Exemption applies whether furnished or unfurnished

exemptions.name: All residents are full-time students
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property occupied only by full-time students (and/or student nurses, foreign spouses/dependants of students, and school/college leavers). Also applies during vacation where students hold a tenancy and intend to return.
exemptions.duration: While all occupiers qualify as students

exemptions.name: Under-18 residents only
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property occupied only by persons under the age of 18. Owner/landlord name appears on the bill as persons under 18 cannot be liable.

exemptions.name: Annexe occupied by dependent relative
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Annexe forming part of a single property, occupied by a dependent relative of the resident in the main dwelling. Dependent relative = aged 65+, or substantially and permanently disabled, or severely mentally impaired.
exemptions.note: Annexe defined as a dwelling forming part of a single property that cannot be let separately without breach of planning controls

exemptions.name: Severely mentally impaired only
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property occupied only by person(s) who are severely mentally impaired and would otherwise be liable for council tax. Exemption also applies where occupied jointly by severely mentally impaired persons and full-time students.
exemptions.note: Liable person must be severely mentally impaired. If someone else is liable (e.g. HMO or care home), exemption does not apply. Doctor certification and qualifying benefit required.

exemptions.name: Visiting forces
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended; Visiting Forces Act 1952, s.12
exemptions.eligibility: Property occupied by a person with a relevant association with a visiting force from a country to which the Visiting Forces Act 1952 applies, or their non-British dependants

exemptions.name: Diplomats and international organisations
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Property where a person who would otherwise be liable is a non-British diplomat or member of an international organisation headquartered in the UK

exemptions.name: Student halls of residence
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Accommodation owned or managed by a prescribed educational establishment and used as student accommodation

exemptions.name: Caravan pitch or boat mooring (unoccupied)
exemptions.legislation: Council Tax (Exempt Dwellings) Order 1992 (SI 1992/558), Article 3, as amended
exemptions.eligibility: Caravan pitch not occupied by a caravan, or boat mooring not occupied by a boat. A pitch or mooring is a dwelling only where used as a person's sole or main residence.

empty_homes.name: Long-term Empty Homes Premium
empty_homes.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992 ss.11B-11C (as amended by Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023)
empty_homes.note: Premium attaches to the property — purchasing an empty property inherits its accumulated premium.

levels.note: Threshold reduced from 2 years to 1 year from 1 April 2025

qualifying_exceptions: Property where occupation is prohibited by planning restrictions
Property held by trustee in bankruptcy
Property of deceased person (for up to 6 months after grant of probate)
Property where owner moved to care home
Property where owner is providing care elsewhere

second_homes.name: Second Homes Premium
second_homes.legislation: Local Government Finance Act 1992 ss.11B-11C (as amended by Levelling-up and Regeneration Act 2023)
second_homes.definition: Furnished property not occupied as any person's sole or main residence
qualifying_exceptions: job_related: 50% discount may apply — see job-related-second-home discount

council_tax_support.name: Council Tax Support
council_tax_support.note: Not a discount. Liability is reduced following a means-tested financial assessment.
council_tax_support.scheme_type: Default Scheme (mirrors original Council Tax Benefit rules with local amendments)

stages.name: Reminder notice
stages.required_response: Bring account up to date within 7 days AND continue with instalments
stages.note: Partial payment does not stop recovery action

stages.name: Second reminder notice
stages.required_response: Bring account up to date within 7 days AND continue with instalments
stages.note: If further instalments missed, no further reminder notices will be issued

stages.name: Final notice
stages.trigger: Failure to bring account up to date within 7 days of second reminder, OR further missed payment in same financial year

stages.name: Liability Order
stages.trigger: Full outstanding amount not paid, OR no payment arrangement agreed
stages.action: Application to Magistrates Court for Liability Order
stages.what_it_is: Court demand for the full amount of Council Tax owed plus costs incurred
stages.note: Do not ignore a Liability Order — further enforcement action will follow

post_liability_order_powers.overview: A Liability Order grants additional recovery powers. The Council will use these proportionately.

enforcement_agent.name: Enforcement agent (bailiffs)
enforcement_agent.description: Enforcement agents instructed to recover money owed plus their additional costs
enforcement_agent.powers: Can take goods belonging to the debtor to cover costs

attachment_of_earnings.name: Deductions from earnings
attachment_of_earnings.description: Employer legally required to deduct money from salary each month and pay directly to Council
attachment_of_earnings.basis: Amount depends on earnings level. Continues until full amount paid.
attachment_of_earnings.employer_obligation: This is a legal order — employer must comply

attachment_of_benefits.name: Deductions from benefits
attachment_of_benefits.description: DWP instructed to take money from eligible benefits and pay directly to Council

charging_order.name: Charging order
charging_order.description: Order means Council Tax debt can be claimed from property equity when property is sold (after mortgage paid)
charging_order.additional_power: Council may be able to insist on sale of property

bankruptcy.name: Bankruptcy
bankruptcy.threshold: Liability Order granted AND debt over £5,000
process: Statutory demand issued — full amount to be paid within 21 days
If unpaid, bankruptcy petition submitted to court
If granted: possible loss of home, bank accounts may be frozen, credit affected, official receiver investigates affairs
bankruptcy.note: Official receiver may control spending for a considerable period

committal.name: Committal (prison)
committal.process: Application to Magistrates Court for committal hearing
committal.important: If committal summons received, contact Council immediately and seek legal advice. Paying in full before hearing means court attendance not required. Failure to attend summons may result in warrant for arrest.

enforcement.vulnerability_principle: Vulnerability and hardship must be actively considered before and throughout any enforcement action.

valuation_band.escalation: Valuation Tribunal for England if unresolved with VOA
